results.title: OptionV: MacOS menubar clipboard manager | Product Hunt
results.content:
I just shipped our biggest OptionV update yet.  
  
When I started building a clipboard manager for macOS, I wanted something that actually fits into a developer's workflow — not just a list of copied items sitting in a corner.  
  
This update is about giving you real control:  
  
→ Full window dashboard — expand from the menubar when you need the full picture  
→ Inline text editing — tweak a snippet before you paste it, right inside the app  
→ Favorites & Collections — organize your most-used code snippets and links  
→ Universal search — fuzzy, instant, across everything  
→ Light & dark modes — because your setup, your rules  
  
Plus a round of performance improvements and sync fixes that have been on the list for a while. [...] OptionV

# OptionV

OptionV

OptionV

@curiouskitty As someone who jumps between Windows and macOS all day, the 'breaking point' for me was muscle memory.I got tired of my brain searching for the Win + V logic on Mac and finding something completely different (or nothing at all). I built this specifically to bridge that gap—bringing that same intuitive, 'at-the-cursor' flow to macOS. If you're looking for that familiar Windows simplicity without the configuration bloat of bigger Mac apps, this is for you.

Davit Svanidze
FlowMarket

FlowMarket

Nice, love the simplicity, fast access to clipboard history right from the menubar just makes sense; how do you handle privacy for sensitive data copied to the clipboard?

Batuhan YILDIRIM

results.title: TimeAlign: Align your time with your goals & optimize your life | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

Jordan J. Dominguez

TimeAlign

TimeAlign

Maker

@tonyhanded Haha oh man, your first bullet point... check this out:  The "prod" version of it is just a custom GPT right now, but we have an alpha version of the feature connected to TimeAlign (thanks to our team wizard, @howljs). SO yes, we've thought about this. To us they are symbiotic, the "life coach"/LLM side helps you break your fuzzy goals and ideas into actionable plans, and then TimeAlign enables you to hold yourself accountable and actualize them.

Report

Jordan J. Dominguez

TimeAlign

TimeAlign

Maker [...] creating a better mobile calendar experience. We are also currently building some of the tracking automations you mentioned (siri integration, auto-categorization, etc) and they should be available in the near future. - Differentiation: this is something we've been working on honing for a long time, so you're feedback is well received. We talked about filming a "day in the life" to show the feature story a bit better. I agree that the messaging can be simplified more and focus on the uniqueness of the product. We will have this messaging (and a nice video) dialed-in by the next launch! [...] Report

results.title: Sliq: Automated data cleaning, in minutes, not hours or days. | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

Marcus Hoang

@jaydev13  Fuzzy matching for categorical data is such an underappreciated problem. State abbreviations are just the tip of the iceberg. Once you get into things like job titles, company names, or product categories the variations are endless. I've seen datasets where the same company appears as 5 different strings. Curious if you ended up finding a good approach for this in your workflow?

Report

Sergei Salii-Luzhevskii

Congrats on the launch! Automated data cleaning is a massive pain killer for DEs.

However, I noticed a critical Signal Dissonance here.

You are building a serious infrastructure tool for engineers, but your visual language signals "Lightweight B2C App for enthusiasts". [...] If you need any other file types or integration that we currently don't support, write to me, and I'll do my best.

To use the library, you first need to get your API key.  
You can find it in the account page of the dashboard.  
  
To get registered for free, sign up here

Happy Cleaning!  
Daniel.

Report

Jay Dev

Wow, Sliq looks amazing! Auto-fixing formats is a game changer. How does it handle inconsistencies in categorical data like state abbreviations, is there a fuzzy matching component?

Report

Daniel

Sliq

Sliq

Maker

@jaydev13 Hi Jay, 👋 Thanks for trying out Sliq! Yes, Sliq can handle inconsistent categorical data, but it uses something else other then just a simple fuzzy matching to improve the quality of the results.

Report
